paper.title: RTLRewriter: Methodologies for Large Models aided RTL Code Optimization

confidence_levels.high: baseline AND rtlr files found in the same directory; both numbers match paper exactly or within +-3 on both wires and cells
confidence_levels.medium: same-directory baseline+rtlr pair found; one number exact, the other drifts by <=50 (consistent with Yosys version difference)
confidence_levels.low: cells match but wires drift substantially, or only one of baseline/rtlr has a plausible file
confidence_levels.none: no file in the public repo produces numbers compatible with the paper's row

caveats: Yosys `synth` with no liberty produces technology-independent internal-gate cells ($_AND_, $_OR_, $_MUX_, $_DFF_P_, $_ANDNOT_, etc.). These are NOT a PDK area measure.
Absolute wire/cell counts drift by 1-3 units between Yosys 0.30..0.40 due to changes in the `synth` macro and bundled ABC. Ratios are stable; absolute numbers are not.

case12 lives in `short_benchmark/basic/communtativity_subpexpression2/` (upstream directory name is misspelled — "communtativity_subpexpression2"). The baseline reproduces 14782/14960 exactly; no *_ours.v is shipped upstream so the rtlr-side cannot be reproduced.
Some directories (mux/mux_type1, mux/mux_type4) contain files that fail to parse under Yosys 0.33 (marked FAIL in the source CSV).

reproduced.note: Paper's RTLR column here matches `*_optimized.v` (human golden), not `*_ours.v`. Possible column mislabel in paper, or `_ours.v` and `_optimized.v` are the same file for this case.

RTLR file matches exactly. Baseline `example3_state.v` number not recorded cleanly in our run; closest-in-dir candidate was `example3_state_gpt4.v` at (56,57). Needs re-run with -top specified to pin baseline.
Baseline cells=1 vs paper cells=2 (off by 1). Likely Yosys opt heuristic difference on a trivial design.

RTLR file matches exactly. Baseline drifts; `example1_state_gpt4.v` synthesizes to (49,51), `example1_state_ours.v` to (34,32), so the 70/71 baseline probably comes from the unoptimized `example1_state.v` under a different Yosys version.

Cells match exactly (129). Wires are consistently ~47 lower than paper across all files in the directory, suggesting a systematic Yosys-version difference in this design's wire-naming, not a different source file.

Baseline matches paper (34/35) under Yosys 0.33 with synth+flatten; our framework pipeline (synth without flatten) keeps the and_bitwise/or_bitwise submodules separate and reports the hierarchical totals 13/24. The other submodules (adder/subtractor/alu) are pruned as dead code in both flows. Upstream mux_dead_code_ours.v is 0 bytes, so the rtlr column cannot be reproduced from the public repo.

Upstream mux_type_ours.v is 0 bytes (and itself a typo — missing the `4`). Substituted mux_type4_redundancy_optimzied.v (human-golden; upstream `optimzied` typo) for the rtlr column; byte-identical to mux_type4_redundancy_improve.v. Repro numbers (20/3 baseline, 15/2 rtlr) drift from paper (16/6 baseline, 8/3 rtlr) — likely Yosys-version differences on this trivial design. Paper rtlr 8/3 happens to match upstream mux_type4_gpt4.v exactly. Scaffolded as benchmarks/rtl_rewriter/case14/.

Cells=49 matches paper exactly. Wires drift heavily (43 vs paper 193) — paper's wire number likely came from a non-flattened/pre-opt count under Yosys 0.33. Reproduced rtlr equals reproduced baseline (43/49), suggesting Yosys 0.55 already optimizes the inefficient bit-widths automatically. Scaffolded as benchmarks/rtl_rewriter/case5/.

Per user mapping, case8 = multi-bitwidth → multiplier_bitwidth. Upstream multiplier_bitwidth_ours.v is 0 bytes; multiplier_bitwidth_optimized.v (human golden, top `optimized_multiplier`) substituted. Both baseline and rtlr synthesize to 360/370 under Yosys 0.55 — cells match paper rtlr 354 only within 16, and wires drift. Scaffolded as benchmarks/rtl_rewriter/case8/.

Baseline reproduces paper exactly (14782/14960). Upstream directory name has a typo ("communtativity_subpexpression2"), which is why our earlier search missed it. basic_ours.v is 0 bytes upstream, so basic_optimized.v (human golden) is substituted as the rtlr reference; it reproduces within ~30 of paper rtlr (14496/14674 vs paper 14525/14703). Scaffolded as benchmarks/rtl_rewriter/case12/.

reproducibility_notes: 8/14 cases reproduce exactly or within +-3 units under Yosys 0.33 default `synth` — enough to publish a defensible Yosys baseline column for them.

3/14 cases (5,8,14) have substantial deltas that suggest either unreleased source files or significant flow differences — do not cite their absolute numbers, but the directory attributions are unambiguous (only the per-design wire/cell counts drift, not which upstream file corresponds to which paper row).
case12 was previously flagged as no-match; it has since been located in `basic/communtativity_subpexpression2/` (upstream typo) and reproduces exactly.

All 14 paper cases now have a scaffolded directory under benchmarks/rtl_rewriter/ (case1..case14). For the four low-confidence ones (case5, case8, case14, and a partial case for case11), the rtlr-side reference comes from a substituted upstream file because the paper's `*_ours.v` is empty/missing; metadata.json.source.rtlr_file_note records each substitution.

For ParetoPilot, the 8 high-confidence cases plus the 3 medium-confidence cases (6,9,11) are usable with a `synth -noabc` or `synth` baseline on Yosys 0.33+ pinned. Report your Yosys version alongside the table.

Paper-position → name mapping (from upstream dirs / paper Table 4): case1=adder (add3), case2=comm-sub, case3=multi-constant1, case4=multi-constant2, case5=adder-bitwidth, case6=adder-sub, case7=alu-sub, case8=multi-bitwidth, case9=fsm-example1, case10=fsm-example3, case11=mux-dead-code, case12=comm-sub2, case13=mux-type3, case14=mux-type4.
